title: Seek Jesus for His Own Sake
subtitle: By the shore at Capernaum, the crowd that ate the loaves finds Him again — and He sees why they came

stillness: Let us learn to stand near to Jesus.
Not for the giving of things felt by the senses,
but to cling without ceasing to the things of the spirit.
Be still, and seek the bread of heaven.
gospel: When the Lord Jesus had crossed over with His disciples from the desert to Gennesareth,
the next day the crowds He had fed did not find Him in the place where He had done this.
Boats came over from Tiberias, near that same desert,
and crossing the sea they came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
And when they found Him across the sea, they marveled how He had come there,
since they had seen only the one small boat, which He had not entered with His disciples.
And they said to Him: Rabbi, when did You come here?
Jesus answered them, not to their question, but to their intention:
Amen, I say to you, you seek Me and follow Me —
not because you saw the signs by which you might have come to know Me,
not for the word you heard,
but because you ate of the loaves and were filled.
Work not for the food that perishes,
but for that which endures unto life everlasting,
which the Son of Man will give you.
behold: Consider now how great the sweetness of those loaves was,
by whose sweetness those crowds were caught, and followed the Lord still, so anxiously.
And no wonder — for sweet and gentle was the provider and dispenser of those loaves.
See how they cross the sea in their boats, seeking Jesus,
and marvel to find Him there, who had entered no boat with them.
But He, who reads not the question of their lips but the intention of their hearts,
answers them: you seek Me, not for My sake, but for the filling of your bellies.
consider: They sought Him for the flesh, not for the spirit — only to be filled again.
Yet they should have been more anxious for the food of the spirit, by which the soul is sustained.
For that food is as much better than bodily food as the soul is known to be better than the body.
And so it is daily: many seek Jesus, but not for the sake of Jesus —
only that it may go well with them in this present life, that they may gain comforts and escape harm.
Many even make Jesus present on the altar, not for Jesus, but for money.
Scarcely is Jesus sought for the sake of Jesus.
Those who in prayer seek not the eternal but the temporal —
they too seek Jesus not for Jesus, but for some other thing.
The food that perishes is worked by everyone who turns a spiritual intention toward temporal gain.
But he who turns bodily labor, through right intention, toward the things of the spirit —
he works the food that does not perish, but endures unto life everlasting.
This food is the food of the Word of God and of grace.
He says: do not labor for the food that perishes — not that you should be idle,
but that you should labor and give; for this is the food that does not perish.
Let us direct our chief study and intention toward seeking the food that leads to life everlasting.
He Himself is this food, by which the angels live, which endures unto life everlasting.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, sufficient salvation of my soul,
grant me to desire You alone,
desiring You for Your own sake;
to seek You, not for any other thing;
and the sins redeemed, not to do again.
Enlighten, I beg You, Lord, my heart with the light of Your divine grace,
that I may have You as my guide in all my ways,
and that I may ever fear and love You above all things, who are above all things,
and do Your will in all things, and never depart from You,
for You alone are enough, and You promise life everlasting,
to which may You mercifully bring me. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quæritis me propter aliud; quærite me propter me, non propter aliud, qui sum cibus permanens in vitam æternam.
latinJewel.english: You seek Me for the sake of something else; seek Me for My own sake, not for anything else — I who am the food that endures unto life everlasting.
